utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check the final coverage plan: it includes health_coverage, property_coverage (null), travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. Next, verify each is fully specified per the format, which requires each to include costs (except total budget which needs monthly/annual breakdown). - health_coverage: includes costs (monthly premium 210, annual 2520) and details; satisfies. - property_coverage: null; not included and lacks costs; does not satisfy. - travel_coverage: includes features but no costs; not fully specified. - additional_services: listed but no costs; not fully specified. - total_budget: includes monthly and annual elements (current monthly spend, new monthly premium, annual savings) but not a complete monthly/annual cost breakdown of all items; partially specified but not fully complete per requirement of 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown'. Therefore, only health_coverage is fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes a health plan explicitly named EliteCare Premium Plan and mentions travel coverage features consistent with travel insurance but no specific product name. Property coverage is null, and additional services listed do not match any named enhancement services from the ground-truth. Therefore, only the explicitly named plan can be mapped to a rating: EliteCare Premium Plan under primary_health_coverage (physical health) has a rating of 10. No other named items from the ground-truth are present.
